gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 initially found that document root exports still fell back to legacy wrappers, then found legacy monkeypatch isolation gaps for dataclass method globals, ephemeral dataclass identity on unpatched calls, and saved-original wrapper recursion. The branch now publishes benchmark-plan symbols from document_kv_cache, clones legacy compatibility globals only when needed, preserves unpatched public dataclass identity/equality/pickling, excludes a wrapper's own name from injected legacy patches, and the final GPT-5.5 review approved the slice.
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_benchmark_plan.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q
repository secret scan over README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, pyproject.toml, src, tests, databricks, pr-evidence, and .github
what_changed: Moved the benchmark-plan implementation into document_kv_cache.benchmark_plan with a curated document-owned public API.
Converted restaurant_kv_serving.benchmark_plan into a compatibility facade that delegates to the document module.
Extended public-package and benchmark-plan tests to cover document root exports, legacy facade behavior, monkeypatch isolation, dataclass identity, pickling, and saved-original wrapper recursion.
why: The package is being generalized from restaurant-specific serving code to document-kv-cache, so benchmark plan ownership needs to live in the document namespace while keeping old imports working during migration.
